description: A designed services-promotion graphic (portrait orientation) for ICE's VOICE office. The right two-thirds is a warm-toned stock photograph of two pairs of hands clasped together in a comforting gesture, with a blurred seated person in a light blue top in the background, suggesting consolation or counseling. A blue translucent panel on the left holds white text: 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by a large bold 'HELP'. An orange angled accent shape sits at the right edge. A white band across the bottom contains the contact info '1-855-48-VOICE' and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE' (with 'VOICE' emphasized in orange) beside the VOICE logo (a stylized blue 'V') and the program name 'VOICE — VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT'. The technique uses an empathetic, supportive image and the word 'HELP' to promote the VOICE program, which frames immigrants as perpetrators by centering victims of 'crime linked to immigration'. Target audience: members of the public who consider themselves victims of crimes by immigrants, plus a broader messaging aim of associating immigration with criminal victimization.
notable_text: If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT
